fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Indiana Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Indiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity indiana, how to transfer dietitian license to indiana, dietitian compact indiana, indiana dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Dietitians Certification Board
board.mailing_address:
402 West Washington Street, Room W072
Indianapolis, IN 46204
board.physical_address:
402 West Washington Street, Room W072

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by Dec 31, even years) + 30 CE hours

license_types.title: Certified Dietitian (CD)
license_types.description: Indiana's state certification for dietitians. While optional for use of the 'Registered Dietitian' title, most employers require both the national RD credential and the state CD certification. Allows use of the 'Certified Dietitian' title.

steps.title: Complete Required Education and Supervised Practice
steps.description: You must complete a didactic program in dietetics and supervised practice accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

steps.title: Pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians
steps.description: You must pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Apply for Certification
steps.description: Submit an application for certification to the Indiana Dietitians Certification Board and pay the required fee.

documents.detail: Sent directly from the university, showing degree in an approved field and date awarded.
Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.
Recent photographs meeting standard passport specifications.

provisional_text: Indiana does not offer a provisional or temporary dietitian certification. Applicants must hold active CDR registration and complete all requirements before certification is issued.
reciprocity_text: Indiana does not have a formal endorsement or reciprocity pathway. Out-of-state dietitians apply through the standard application process. Holding current CDR registration satisfies the education and examination requirements. You must still submit the application, $20 fee, transcripts, and complete the Indiana fingerprint background check.

faqs.question: How much does an Indiana dietitian certification cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee is only $20 — one of the lowest in the country. The biennial renewal is also $20 (plus a $10 late fee if submitted after December 31). Fingerprint background check fees are paid separately to the vendor.

faqs.question: Is Indiana dietitian certification required to practice?
faqs.answer: Indiana's Certified Dietitian (CD) credential is technically optional for practicing as a Registered Dietitian (RD). However, most employers in Indiana require both the national RD credential and the state CD certification.

faqs.question: What continuing education is required in Indiana?
faqs.answer: Indiana requires 30 hours of continuing education during each two-year licensure period for active renewal. Continuing education is not required for inactive renewal.

faqs.question: Does Indiana have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Indiana. To work in Indiana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Indiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Indiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Indiana does not have a formal endorsement or reciprocity pathway. Out-of-state dietitians apply through the standard application process. Holding current CDR registration satisfies the education and examination requirements. You must still submit the application, $20 fee, transcripts, and complete the Indiana fingerprint background check.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Indiana
hero.dek: Indiana Dietitian certification requires completion of academic requirements, passing the Registration Examination for Dietitians, and paying the required fee.
